raw_comment: Wow! This is the first interactive graph I have seen on this learning platform. It is quite fitting since spring has sprung! I really enjoy this visual because it led me to notice that the areas of the United States that are colored first are closest to the equator. I wonder why the purple colored areas have leaves appear after the the yellow colored areas, considering they are lower, closer to the green colored states. I also am curious about why the West Coast has such varied colors. I never realized it took approximately five months for all 50 states to experience spring, with its fullest leaves. May 31- in my New Jersey-mindset- is closer to summer. A catchy phrase for this time lapse could be "The Coloring of America: A 5 Month Process".
ace_sentences: This is the first interactive graph that I have seen on this learning platform.
This visual led me to notice something.
The areas of the United States are colored first.
These areas are closest to the equator.
The purple colored areas have leaves appear after the yellow colored areas.
The purple colored areas are lower.
The purple colored areas are closer to the green colored states.
I wonder why the purple colored areas have leaves appear after the yellow colored areas.
I am curious about why the West Coast has such varied colors.
It took approximately five months for all 50 states to experience spring.
All 50 states experience spring with its fullest leaves.
May 31 is closer to summer in my New Jersey-mindset-.
A catchy phrase for this time lapse could be "The Coloring of America: A 5 Month Process".
